items.headline: City updates youth services and library facility plans
items.summary: San José City Council reviewed progress on its Children and Youth Services Master Plan, including programs like the Safe Summer Initiative and Youth Intervention Services. The council also heard updates on the Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan, which outlines improvements to library facilities across the city.

items.headline: Historic downtown building gets bonus floor space for upgrades
items.summary: The city is considering elevating 405 Kipling Street from a Category 4 to Category 2 historic resource, which would allow the owner to build an additional 5,000 square feet in exchange for historic and seismic rehabilitation work. This is a quasi-judicial hearing where the outcome will be decided.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed for San Antonio Road
items.summary: A developer is seeking to rezone and redevelop property at 788-790 and 796 San Antonio Road into an eight-story building with 167 residential units (including 28 affordable units) and 1,400 square feet of commercial space. The project requires City Council approval and represents significant change to that corridor.

items.headline: 333 Franklin Street zoning hearing postponed indefinitely
items.summary: A zoning hearing for a project at 333 Franklin Street was cancelled on April 8, 2026, with no new hearing date yet scheduled. The project will need to be re-noticed once the city sets a new date.

items.headline: Major Automation Park expansion signals tech sector growth
items.summary: A $7.4M tenant improvement at 1768 Automation Parkway suggests significant corporate expansion in San José's key tech corridor. This large-scale commercial project indicates continued demand for office space from growing companies.

items.headline: City Council Approves Construction Contract with QLM, Inc.
items.summary: The Town Manager was authorized to execute a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the specific project details are not fully described in the agenda. Residents should watch for follow-up information about what construction work this contract covers and where it will take place in Los Gatos.

items.headline: Grant awarded for Mexican Heritage Plaza School of Arts and Culture
items.summary: San José received a California Natural Resources Agency grant to support the School of Arts and Culture at Mexican Heritage Plaza. The council also approved a contract amendment for the Bascom Avenue traffic signal project and awarded a pavement repair contract for local streets.

items.headline: City Council updates Active Transportation Plan with revised criteria
items.summary: The council reviewed Phase 2 of Cupertino's Active Transportation Plan, including edits and new scoring criteria that will shape how the city prioritizes biking and pedestrian infrastructure. This plan directly affects where sidewalk improvements, bike lanes, and pedestrian safety upgrades get built.
